decision_summary: The chip max temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. The heat spreader conductivity is currently at its upper bound (500.0), so we will increase the base plate conductivity to improve downstream heat extraction toward the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Doubling the base plate conductivity within the allowed 2.0x ratio will reduce thermal resistance between the heat spreader and the side walls held at 25°C, facilitating better heat removal.

expected_effects: Improve lateral heat flow through the base layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns if the bottleneck remains strictly within the spreader or chip interface
Base material conductivity is now at a moderate level; further increases may require geometry adjustments in future steps
